about_us_title: Project Bruut: By and for the community
about_us_intro: Robotics in agriculture is often complex and unaffordable. We believe this can be different. Project Bruut was born from the desire to design a modular, open-source field robot that you, as a farmer or maker, can build, understand, and adapt yourself.
about_us_opensource_text: We want to make complex robotics accessible by providing a solid foundation. Because the project is open-source, you are not tied to expensive licenses or closed ecosystems. You have the freedom to fully customize the tool carrier to your own needs and share your innovations with others.
about_us_independence_text: No unnecessary electronics or overly complex systems. We use as many off-the-shelf standard components as possible. This means the robot is easy to build and, more importantly, that you can repair it yourself in the field without having to wait for an expensive technician.
about_us_reality_text: Let’s be honest: robotics is difficult. So don’t expect a ready-made commercial product wrapped up with a bow. What we do offer is a robust, programmable frame and a clear manual. We give you the tool—you give the robot its purpose.
about_us_community_text: Whether you’re a farmer with a welding machine or a developer who enjoys working with Ardupilot—we do this together. How great is it to share ideas as a community of enthusiasts, improve them, test them in the mud, and simply use them together?
about_us_conclusion: Help build the future of accessible agricultural robotics. Share your attachments, improve the code, or simply start building your own Bruut.
title: Welcome to Project Bruut
subtitle: Open-source robotics for the agricultural sector
about_text: Why open-source? Because we want to advance the agricultural sector with shared knowledge and modular technology.
footer_tagline: Simple agricultural robotics for everyone. Modular, electric, and fully open-source.
footer_copyright: © {year} Open Source Project Bruut. All rights reserved.
front_page_hero_subtitle: An open-source platform accessible to farmers, makers, and enthusiasts.
front_page_feature_1_desc: No unnecessary electronics and use of off-the-shelf components. The robot is easy to build, repair, and expand.
front_page_feature_2_desc: Lightweight design with adjustable width and length via a hole-bar system. By mirroring sides, the width is easily scalable for open fields, greenhouses, or vegetable gardens.
front_page_feature_3_desc: Choose from 2 hub motors with rear caster wheels, or 4 hub motors for maximum traction. Power ranges from 400 watts to 5 kW, depending on the application.
front_page_info_platform_desc: The goal is to make complex robotics accessible by providing a solid foundation. This allows you to build specific applications and attachments yourself and control them independently.
front_page_media_desc: Watch how the modular field robot moves across the land. Fully electric and ready for the future.
front_page_project_build_desc: Learn how to build the modular field robot yourself step by step using standard components.
front_page_project_goal_desc: Discover the vision behind this project and why we are making complex agricultural robotics open-source.
front_page_project_functions_desc: View the technical specifications, control systems, and the possibilities for various attachments.
func_hero_subtitle: The Bruut platform is a versatile tool carrier. Discover all modules and attachments you can add for specific tasks in the field, greenhouse, or yard.
func_1_desc: Mounted push blade at the front pushing feed/ grass in barns. Or for cleaning the erf and feed silo's
func_2_desc: Large front-mounted brush or an electrically driven radial brush for cleaning surfaces.
func_3_desc: Mowing at different heights, including 'topping' plants above ground level.
func_4_desc: Spotspray boom for spraying with AI for only the weeds, for reducing the chemicals and less drift
func_5_desc: Power source within the frame for additional power or electricity generation.
func_6_desc: Pumping water from ditches for irrigation or crop spraying.
func_7_desc: Seat mounted at the front or rear, with the option to widen the frame tubes on the sides.
func_8_desc: Digging trenches to drain standing water from fields into ditches.
func_9_desc: Integrated panels to extend battery life during operation.
func_10_desc: Even distribution of sawdust or bedding material.
func_12_desc: Machine for sowing crops or overseeding existing grassland.
func_13_desc: Transporting and processing compost within the frame.
func_14_desc: Wheelbarrow-style container for transporting loose materials.
func_16_desc: Ballast weights to improve traction and balance of the vehicle.
func_17_desc: Spreading lime to improve soil pH levels.
func_18_desc: Flexible arm with a brush cutter or blades for mowing under fences and around obstacles.
func_19_desc: Spreading straw, specially designed for use between vegetable rows.
func_20_desc: Assistance with lifting and transport, functioning as a mini dumper.
func_21_desc: External hydraulic power source for applications where hydraulics are preferred over electricity.
func_22_desc: Standard lifting system for attaching various agricultural implements.
func_23_desc: Soil cultivation specifically for vegetable gardens or light plots.
func_24_desc: Mechanical weed control using rotating fingers in light soil.
func_25_desc: Targeted weed control based on the 'Open Weed Locator' project.
func_26_desc: Protective enclosure or cabin for the operator.
func_27_desc: Improving soil structure and air supply in grassland.
func_28_desc: Measuring crop height to create task maps (e.g. for nitrogen or phosphate).
func_29_desc: Measuring and mapping soil density at specific data points.
goal_hero_subtitle: Making complex robotics accessible by providing a solid foundation, powered by an active community of farmers and makers.
goal_feature1_desc: A platform where makers and enthusiasts share, improve, and test their designs, code, and attachments.
goal_feature2_desc: Thanks to electric drive and solar panel options, the robot is ideal for emission-free work in greenhouses and in the field.
goal_feature3_desc: Lighter and more efficient work for agricultural entrepreneurs, from large-scale farming to smaller vegetable gardens.
goal_info_title: From Base Frame to Your Own Application
goal_info_desc: We provide the tools and the setup. The farmer or maker then gives it a purpose, with independent control for endless possibilities.
robotbuild_hero_title: Build Your Own Bruut Field Robot
robotbuild_hero_subtitle: A step-by-step guide to assembling the modular chassis and drive system.
robotbuild_chassis_desc: The frame is constructed from standard hole-pattern beams, making width and length easily adjustable for any application.
robotbuild_drive_desc: Hub motors provide direct traction in the field. Available in configurations from 400W to 5kW.
robotbuild_power_desc: Modular battery system with solar panel options for long-term autonomous field use.
robotbuild_control_desc: Full control via ESP32 or Raspberry Pi controllers, optimized for easy programming.
